title: LLM Evaluation (DeepEval)
description: Prove an LLM/RAG/agent feature is correct with numbers a release can gate on — author eval suites in DeepEval with the metric definitions baked in: faithfulness and answer relevancy (generator), contextual precision/recall/relevancy (retriever), hallucination against ground truth, tool-call correctness for agents, plus custom G-Eval criteria, bias, and toxicity. Builds a versioned golden dataset FIRST, picks the metric that matches the actual failure mode (RAG hallucination -> faithfulness; retrieval miss -> contextual recall; wrong tool -> tool correctness), and turns every threshold into a pytest / `deepeval test run` exit code so a regression blocks the pipeline. Enforces guardrails against unpinned LLM-as-judge models, exact-string-matching non-deterministic output, contaminated goldens that leak the prompt's own examples, single-run scores with no variance, averaging unrelated metrics into one number that hides a failing dimension, and confusing the minimize-metrics (hallucination/bias/toxicity, pass when score <= threshold) with the maximize-metrics (everything else, pass when score >= threshold).
